commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=40fcccb832395f2a0be7ff8b999dc46b3648c3b7)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds structured Doctor findings and dry-run repair effects for extra gateway services and stale session locks, threads `--deep` through lint, and skips legacy-owned checks from broad structured repair mutation.

PR surface: Source +169, Tests +243. Total +412 across 12 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review finding by source inspection: registering `core/doctor/session-locks` makes the existing doctor-lint unknown-id test select a real check instead of emitting `core/doctor/lint-selection`. I did not run tests because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: The branch is draft/protected and missing contributor real behavior proof; automation could update the stale test, but it cannot supply the contributor's real setup proof or make the maintainer compatibility/session-state decision.

target.title: Doctor: expose session lock findings
target.body:
## Summary

- Exposes stale session lock files as structured doctor health findings.
- Adds dry-run repair effects for stale lock cleanup without moving real deletion into the structured repair runner.
- Keeps the existing positional Session locks doctor flow as the only mutating cleanup path for openclaw doctor --fix.

This is the small version of the old session repair PR. It does not migrate session transcripts, snapshots, state providers, heartbeat repair, or state integrity repair. Those are user-data-adjacent and can be handled in later PRs only where the structured detector is cheap and the mutation path can stay isomorphic.

## Verification

- pnpm install
- node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/commands/doctor-session-locks.ts src/commands/doctor-session-locks.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.ts
- node_modules/.bin/oxlint src/commands/doctor-session-locks.ts src/commands/doctor-session-locks.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.ts
- git diff --check
- node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor-session-locks.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts --reporter=dot - 2 files, 14 tests
- node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts --reporter=dot - 1 file, 21 tests
- node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --noEmit --incremental false --pretty false
- node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --noEmit --incremental false --pretty false
- bash /mnt/c/src/claws-hapi/.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base fork/doctor-gateway-services-structured-repairs - clean after fixing accepted env-scoping finding

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: openclaw doctor --lint can report stale session lock files as core/doctor/session-locks findings, and dry-run structured repair can describe the stale-lock cleanup effect without deleting user state.
Real environment tested: WSL Ubuntu 24.04, Node 24.15.0, OpenClaw source checkout stacked on #84340.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: focused session-lock/doctor registry Vitest files, focused oxfmt/oxlint, core tsgo, core test tsgo, and autoreview as listed above.
Evidence after fix: focused Vitest passed with 3 files and 35 tests total; both tsgo lanes exited 0; autoreview reported no accepted/actionable findings after env-scoped state-dir detection was fixed.
Observed result after fix: stale session locks are registered as core/doctor/session-locks; structured lint can see path-scoped findings; structured dry-run can describe would-remove-stale-session-lock effects; broad structured fix skips this id so existing positional session-lock cleanup remains the only real mutating path.
What was not tested: live recovery from a real wedged OpenClaw session was not run because this PR preserves the existing cleanup command path and only adds structured reporting around the stale-lock detector.
target.author: giodl73-repo

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
